champion.narrative: No champion is identified. The Salesforce field X10b_Champion_Business_Buyer__c returned no value, and no contact role of Champion exists. The single contact, Meneleo (Jon) Avila (Associate Director, Procurement - Category Lead, Clinical), holds an Evaluator role, not Champion. An evaluator can assess and recommend but typically lacks the influence to drive the buying decision, introduce us to the Economic Buyer or Decision Maker, or advocate for Zycus against three competitors (GEP, Coupa, JAGGAER). No evidence of advocacy exists: no introductions arranged, no internal documents shared, no pushback on competitors on our behalf. The last Salesforce activity was 23 days ago (2026-06-11), and zero buyer calls have been recorded, so current engagement with this contact is passive at best. For a deal with three competitors and no senior-level access, lack of a champion is a critical vulnerability. If the evaluator is benchmarking all four vendors neutrally (as an RFP process would require), we have no internal advocate to position us favorably with the decision committee. Developing this evaluator contact into a champion (by demonstrating unique value in the demo, aligning Zycus capabilities to his personal success metrics, and earning his trust as a strategic partner, not just a vendor) is the foundation move. Simultaneously, we must ask him to introduce us to the senior procurement leader or budget owner, positioning it as 'ensuring we address executive priorities in our demo.'

competition.narrative: Three competitors are confirmed: Coupa, GEP Worldwide, and JAGGAER, documented in the SF Competitors__c field. This signals a formal multi-vendor evaluation, likely an RFP, where the buyer is benchmarking sourcing platforms. Coupa is the incumbent leader in Source-to-Pay, expensive, and sets a high feature and price bar; if the buyer is cost-sensitive or dissatisfied with Coupa's complexity/cost, Zycus can position as the high-value alternative with faster time-to-value and Merlin AI differentiation. GEP Worldwide is peer-priced to Zycus, strong in direct procurement, and a close fit for pharma; GEP is the most dangerous competitor here, as they will match us on price and pharma references, so we must win on ease of use, AI-driven automation (Merlin eSourcing agent), and implementation speed. JAGGAER is strong in public sector and indirect procurement, less differentiated for pharma clinical sourcing, so likely the weakest of the three. The wedge: position Zycus iSource + Merlin as the only platform purpose-built for pharma sourcing complexity (clinical trial materials, regulatory compliance, supplier risk for life sciences) with AI negotiation and autonomous RFx workflows that reduce cycle time by 40-50% vs manual Coupa or GEP processes. No buyer calls exist to confirm competitive sentiment or how the buyer is scoring us vs. the three, so the demo and subsequent RFP response are our first opportunities to differentiate. The immediate move is to ask the evaluator contact, before the demo, which of the three competitors they have already demoed or are leaning toward, and what they liked/disliked, so we can tailor our demo to exploit GEP and Coupa weaknesses.

However, this is inference, not confirmed pain. We do not know the pain owner (is it this Associate Director, or a VP Procurement, or the CFO concerned about clinical trial budget overruns?), the urgency (is there a clinical trial launch or budget review forcing a decision?), or the quantified impact (how much time/cost is the current process costing them?). Moves deal from passive/email-only engagement to active two-way dialogue.

items.action: Ask Meneleo (Jon) Avila to introduce Zycus to the senior procurement leader (VP Procurement or Director, Procurement) and the Economic Buyer (the person who will approve the $50k budget and sign the contract). Position the request as 'We want to ensure our demo and proposal address the priorities of all stakeholders, including senior leadership. Can you introduce us to [senior procurement leader] and [budget approver] so we can briefly align before the demo?' Schedule a 15-minute pre-demo alignment call with the senior leader if the introduction is successful.
items.trigger: Single-thread risk: only 1 evaluator-level contact after 39 days; no Economic Buyer or Decision Maker identified; deal cannot progress past demo into shortlist or negotiation without EB sponsorship.
items.expected_effect: Breaks single-thread risk, maps the Economic Buyer and senior decision-maker, establishes executive-level relationship before commercial negotiation stage, and signals to the evaluator that Zycus is serious and executive-ready (builds his confidence in recommending us). If the evaluator cannot or will not make the introduction, that is a red flag that he lacks influence or we lack his trust, which must be addressed before investing in the demo.

items.action: Confirm demo videos sent to Meneleo (Jon) Avila by 2026-07-08 (4 days from today), and schedule the tailored live eSourcing demo for the week of 2026-07-14 (10 days from today). After the discovery call (rank-1 move), customize the demo to address his quantified pain, showcase Merlin AI eSourcing agent (if AI/automation is a priority), and explicitly compare Zycus advantages vs. GEP Worldwide and Coupa (ease of use, implementation speed, pharma-specific sourcing workflows). Invite IT and Finance stakeholders to the demo if identified during the discovery call, to begin multi-threading.
items.trigger: Demo videos promised 16 days ago (SF Next_Step__c 2026-06-18: 'send demo videos and schedule a tailored live eSourcing demonstration in the coming weeks'); no confirmation of delivery or demo scheduling. If not delivered/scheduled within the next 7 days, buyer may perceive Zycus as slow and favor a faster-moving competitor.
items.expected_effect: Fulfills the commitment to the buyer, prevents perception of Zycus as unresponsive, differentiates Zycus vs. three competitors (GEP, Coupa, JAGGAER) through tailored value demonstration, and creates the forum to begin multi-threading (if IT/Finance invited). A strong demo, anchored to quantified pain and competitive positioning, is the first major milestone toward shortlist and the foundation for developing the evaluator into a champion.

items.action: Prepare a competitive battle card for GEP Worldwide (the most dangerous competitor: peer-priced, pharma-strong) before the demo. Document GEP's weaknesses (longer implementation timeline, manual workflows vs. Merlin AI automation, less ease-of-use) and Zycus's wedge (Merlin eSourcing negotiation agent, 40-50% faster RFx cycle time, 8-10 week implementation vs. GEP's 4-6 months, purpose-built for pharma clinical sourcing). During the demo, explicitly position these advantages when showcasing Merlin AI and iSource workflows. After the demo, ask the evaluator for feedback: 'How does what you've seen from Zycus compare to GEP and Coupa?' to gauge competitive standing.
items.trigger: Three competitors active (GEP, Coupa, JAGGAER) per SF Competitors__c 2026-06-18; no competitive review, positioning session, or intelligence on buyer's scoring of competitors. GEP Worldwide is peer-priced and pharma-strong, making it the most direct threat.
items.expected_effect: Equips the deal team to differentiate Zycus during the demo and post-demo follow-up, positions Merlin AI as the wedge vs. GEP's manual workflows, and extracts competitive sentiment from the buyer (are we winning, losing, or neutral vs. GEP/Coupa?) so we can adjust positioning before the RFP response or shortlist decision. A well-executed competitive demo can move Zycus from 'one of four' to 'top two' in the buyer's mind.

items.action: After the discovery call and demo, if the evaluator contact (Meneleo Avila) demonstrates trust and alignment (shares internal priorities, provides candid feedback on competitors, facilitates introductions to senior stakeholders), begin developing him into a champion. Schedule a follow-up 1:1 call to align Zycus's value proposition to his personal success metrics (e.g., 'If you select Zycus, what outcomes would make you a hero internally? Faster RFx cycle time? Cost savings? Easier user adoption?'), offer to co-create the internal business case or ROI model he will present to senior leadership, and ask him to advocate for Zycus in internal vendor discussions. Position Zycus as his strategic partner, not just a vendor. If he is unwilling or unable to advocate, reassess whether we have the right primary contact or whether we need to escalate engagement to a senior procurement leader who can champion us.
items.trigger: No champion identified after 39 days (SF OpportunityContactRole: only Evaluator role, no Champion role; zero Avoma buyer calls showing advocacy). For a deal with three competitors and no senior-level access, lack of a champion is a critical vulnerability.
items.expected_effect: Converts the evaluator contact from neutral assessor to internal advocate, giving Zycus a voice inside the buying committee when we are not in the room. A champion can position us favorably vs. GEP/Coupa, navigate internal politics, introduce us to the EB, and push back on competitor objections. Champion development is the foundation for winning a multi-competitor deal. If champion development fails, it signals we lack differentiation or trust, which must be addressed before commercial negotiation or we will lose on price.

north_star_verdict.headline: Early-stage deal with 228 days runway but critically single-threaded through one evaluator-level contact. No Economic Buyer, Decision Maker, or champion mapped after 39 days. Three named competitors (GEP, Coupa, JAGGAER) suggest a formal multi-vendor RFP, yet we lack access to the buying committee, decision criteria, or timeline. Last Salesforce activity 23 days ago; no buyer calls discovered. Demo videos promised but not yet confirmed delivered. Deal can stall indefinitely in Qualified without senior sponsorship and multi-threading.

flags: Multi-thread immediately during demo scheduling: only 1 buyer contact role on the opportunity (Associate Director Procurement). Pharma procurement decisions involve IT (integration), Finance (budget/ROI), and senior procurement leadership (sign-off). Request that the demo include the procurement director, IT stakeholder, and a finance representative to broaden the stakeholder map and surface the Economic Buyer.

Map Economic Buyer and Decision Maker during the demo conversation: ask the evaluator contact who will approve the budget, sign the contract, and make the final vendor selection. Secure an introduction to that executive sponsor within 14 days of the demo. Without EB access, forecast defensibility collapses in later stages.

Define the competitive wedge in the demo: GEP Worldwide is the peer threat, Coupa the enterprise standard, JAGGAER the third alternative. The demo must uncover which rival the buyer prefers, what each has promised, and where Zycus differentiates. Position iSource on ease of use, AI-driven sourcing intelligence, and faster time-to-value versus GEP complexity and Coupa cost. Make the demo a fit proof, not a feature tour.

Single-thread risk: only 1 buyer-side contact role (Associate Director Procurement, Evaluator level) after 39 days. No IT, Finance, Legal, or senior procurement contacts. If this contact leaves, changes role, or loses influence, we lose the deal. Immediate move: ask the evaluator to introduce us to the senior procurement leader and IT/Finance stakeholders, positioning it as ensuring we address all stakeholder priorities in the demo.

No quantified business case or pain metrics. Without a documented ROI, cost-savings target, or cycle-time reduction goal, the buyer has no financial justification to buy and we have no anchor for pricing negotiation or value demonstration. Three competitors (GEP, Coupa, JAGGAER) will compete on price and ROI; Zycus must quantify value to win. Immediate move: run a 30-minute discovery call with the evaluator before the demo to extract and quantify the specific pain (e.g., 'How much time does your team spend on manual RFx processes per quarter?' 'What is your clinical sourcing spend and target savings?'), then frame the demo as the solution to that quantified problem.

Zero buyer calls discovered after 39 days in Qualified stage. No discovery session, no voice-of-customer, no relationship depth. All engagement is Salesforce-activity-only (emails, tasks). For a deal with three competitors and a tailored demo promised, lack of live conversation is a vulnerability. Immediate move: prioritize a live discovery call before sending demo videos, to uncover pain, criteria, competitive sentiment, and decision process, so the demo can be truly tailored.

Demo videos promised ('in the coming weeks' per Next_Step__c 2026-06-18) but no confirmation of delivery or scheduling of live demo. 16 days since the commitment; if videos are not sent and demo not scheduled within the next 7 days, the evaluator may perceive Zycus as slow to respond and favor a competitor who moved faster. Immediate move: confirm demo videos sent by 2026-07-08 and live demo scheduled for the week of 2026-07-14 or earlier.

Three competitors (Coupa, GEP Worldwide, JAGGAER) active, but no competitive review, positioning session, or battle-card preparation documented. No buyer calls to extract competitive sentiment (which vendors has the buyer already demoed? what did they like/dislike?). Without competitive intelligence, we risk a generic demo that does not differentiate against GEP or Coupa. Immediate move: before the demo, ask the evaluator which competitors they have already seen and what stood out (positive or negative), then tailor the demo to exploit those gaps.

competitive_position.summary: Competing against three established sourcing platforms: Coupa (expensive incumbent leader), GEP Worldwide (peer-priced, pharma-strong, most dangerous), and JAGGAER (public sector focus, less pharma-differentiated). Formal multi-vendor evaluation likely underway. No buyer calls or competitive sentiment on record. Zycus must differentiate on pharma-specific sourcing complexity, Merlin AI automation (negotiation agent, autonomous RFx), and value per dollar vs. Coupa's cost and GEP's feature parity. Demo is the first differentiation opportunity.
